input_evidence.title: Battery Warning Feature Description
input_evidence.text: When the vehicle battery state of charge becomes low, the rider shall be warned on the display. The warning shall remain visible until the battery level is restored or the vehicle is powered off.
BatteryWarningController::update() checks batterySocPercent and activates LOW_BATTERY_TELLTALE when batterySocPercent < configuredLowBatteryThreshold.

expected_artifacts.statement: The rider shall be warned when the vehicle battery state of charge is low.
The system shall display a low-battery warning telltale when the battery state of charge is below the configured low-battery threshold.
expected_artifacts.verification_criteria: Set battery state of charge below the configured threshold and verify that the low-battery telltale is displayed.

expected_artifacts.title: Verify low-battery warning telltale
test_steps: Set the simulated battery state of charge below the threshold.
Confirm that the low-battery telltale is visible.
expected_artifacts.expected_result: The low-battery telltale is displayed while the battery state of charge is below the configured threshold.

expected_gap_checks.description: Every SYS.2 requirement must have source evidence.
Every SYS.2 requirement must have verification criteria or a linked test case.
Safety relevance should not remain TBD before approval.

input_evidence.title: Display Menu Requirements
input_evidence.text: The rider can open the Settings menu from the main display screen. The menu shall allow navigation between Display, Connectivity, and Vehicle Info sections.
MenuRouter maps SETTINGS_ROOT to DISPLAY_SETTINGS, CONNECTIVITY_SETTINGS, and VEHICLE_INFO. Navigation events are handled by handleMenuInput().

expected_artifacts.statement: The rider shall be able to access and navigate the Settings menu from the main display screen.
The system shall provide a Settings menu accessible from the main display screen with Display, Connectivity, and Vehicle Info sections.
expected_artifacts.verification_criteria: Open the Settings menu from the main display screen and verify that Display, Connectivity, and Vehicle Info sections are available.

expected_artifacts.statement: The menu routing software shall map the Settings root menu to Display Settings, Connectivity Settings, and Vehicle Info menu states.

expected_gap_checks.description: Every SYS.2 requirement must have source evidence.
System requirements implemented in software should be linked to SWE.1 requirements.
Every SYS.2 requirement should be linked to a test case.

input_evidence.title: Diagnostic Fault Handling
input_evidence.text: If a display communication fault is detected, the rider shall be informed by a warning state. The system shall avoid showing stale critical values as valid values.
DisplayComMonitor reports DISPLAY_COM_LOSS when no valid display communication frame is received within the configured timeout. The UI enters degraded mode.

expected_artifacts.statement: The rider shall be informed when a display communication fault prevents reliable presentation of information.

The system shall enter a display warning or degraded state when display communication is lost for longer than the configured timeout and shall prevent stale critical values from being presented as valid.
expected_artifacts.verification_criteria: Inject a display communication loss longer than the configured timeout and verify that the system enters the warning or degraded state and does not present stale critical values as valid.

expected_artifacts.statement: The display communication monitoring software shall detect missing valid display communication frames within the configured timeout and request degraded UI mode.

expected_gap_checks.description: Safety-relevant requirements should define a fault reaction or degraded behavior.
Safety-relevant requirements must have verification criteria.
Safety-relevant requirements should be reviewed before approval.

title: Change request impact analysis for warning threshold

input_evidence.title: Change low battery threshold
input_evidence.text: The low-battery warning threshold shall be changed from 20 percent to 15 percent to reduce unnecessary rider warnings.

input_evidence.title: Baseline 1.0 requirements
input_evidence.text: SYS-REQ-001 defines the low-battery warning behavior. SYS-TC-001 verifies low-battery warning telltale activation.

expected_artifacts.statement: Change the low-battery warning threshold from 20 percent to 15 percent.

expected_artifacts.reason: The threshold change affects the triggering condition of the low-battery warning and its verification procedure.

expected_gap_checks.description: Every change request should have impact analysis.
Impacted tests should be identified for requirement changes.

title: Requirement with missing source evidence and missing verification

input_evidence.title: Unverified display requirement note
input_evidence.text: The display should probably show a special service mode indicator.

expected_artifacts.statement: The system shall display a service mode indicator when service mode is active.

expected_gap_checks.description: Every SYS.2 requirement should be derived from stakeholder input or approved source evidence.
expected_gap_checks.expected_gap: The requirement is based only on an informal note and has no approved stakeholder or system source.

expected_gap_checks.description: Every SYS.2 requirement must have verification criteria or a linked test case.
expected_gap_checks.expected_gap: No verification criteria and no linked test case are provided.

expected_gap_checks.description: Safety relevance should be classified before approval.
